phrasal_verbs.meaning: make sb not want to do or not like sth
be almost a particular time, number, age, etc
continue happening or doing sth; do sth after doing sth else
begin to like; begin to do sth regularly
go/come to sb's house to visit them
develop in a particular way or have a particular result
appear unexpectedly or without making a firm arrangement

A.title: Cooking verbs
A.instructions: Complete each sentence with a cooking verb from the box.

questions.sentence: ...... the tofu cubes in a little oil until they are golden and crispy on all sides.
...... the garlic cloves roughly and add them to the pan with the olive oil.
...... the bread rolls in a preheated oven at 200°C for about fifteen minutes.
...... some fresh ginger into the stir-fry to add extra warmth and flavour.
...... the cream until it is thick enough to hold its shape when you lift the beaters.
...... the courgettes thinly and arrange them in overlapping layers in the baking dish.
...... the softened butter and sugar together until the mixture is pale and fluffy.
...... the whole chicken at 200°C for an hour and a half until the skin is golden.
...... the halloumi slices for two to three minutes on each side until lightly charred.
...... the risotto gently over a low heat, adding a ladle of warm stock at a time.
...... the eggs for six minutes if you prefer a soft, runny yolk.
She loves exploring Thai and Japanese **kitchen / cuisine** whenever she travels through Southeast Asia.
**Frozen / Freezing** peas are a convenient and nutritious alternative to fresh ones.
He's the most talented **cooker / cook** I know – everything he makes tastes absolutely incredible.
Once the **kettle / teapot** has boiled, pour the water carefully over the ground coffee in the filter.
Excuse me, could we see the **catalogue / menu** for the set lunch, please?
Gently heat the milk in a small saucepan on the **grill / hob / oven** until it just begins to simmer.
Any batch-cooked meals stored in the **fridge / freezer** will keep for up to three months.
My parents are thinking of replacing their old gas **chef / cooker** with a modern induction model.
Paella and gazpacho are two of Spain's most internationally famous **plates / bowls / saucers / dishes**.
They've booked a table at a rooftop restaurant for a **dinner / lunch** party to celebrate their anniversary.
My flatmate is a committed **vegetable / vegetarian / vegan**, so she avoids all animal products, including honey and wool.
It was getting late, so we decided to order a Thai **takeaway / fast** food rather than cook from scratch.
**Fizzy / Soft** drinks can erode tooth enamel because the carbonation makes them acidic.

C.title: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)
C.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: I've taken (1) ...... calling old friends more often since I started working from home.
Last month, two former classmates had come (2) ...... for coffee and mentioned that Jamie had moved to a new flat in the city.
On a whim, we decided to drop (3) ...... and surprise him with a bottle of wine.
Jamie wasn't home – always a risk when you (4) ...... up unannounced, of course – so his flatmate let us wait inside.
Having left home at noon, it was getting (5) ...... for three in the afternoon before Jamie finally arrived.
While we were waiting in the entrance hall, we ran (6) ...... a former colleague who happened to live in the same building.
Despite the somewhat chaotic beginning, the whole afternoon turned (7) ...... to be really enjoyable.
We noticed that the milk in the fridge had (8) ...... off, so we offered to pop out and get some fresh.
When we got back, Jamie mentioned he had also (9) ...... out of teabags.
He went (10) ...... to explain that he hadn't had time to do a proper food shop since moving in.
He's always keen to try (11) ...... new hobbies – that month he had taken up home baking.
His younger sister kept (12) ...... interrupting our conversation to show us videos on her phone.
I'll leave (13) ...... any description of how chaotic the kitchen actually looked by the end of the visit!
I wouldn't want to (14) ...... anyone off going to visit Jamie, though – he really is a wonderfully generous host.

D.title: Matching (Where do these texts appear?)
D.instructions: Match each text (1-10) with the place where it appears (A-J).

questions.sentence: The grill element must never be operated with the oven door fully closed, as this can cause the appliance to overheat. **C** in the instructions for an electric cooker
Slow-braised lamb shank with rosemary jus, creamy mashed potato and roasted cherry tomatoes. **F** on a menu
Peel and dice the butternut squash. Set aside. Fry the onion and garlic in olive oil over a medium heat until softened. **D** in a recipe book
Open for lunch and dinner seven days a week. Ideal for hen nights, retirement dinners and milestone birthdays. **G** in an advertisement for a restaurant

Place large pots and baking trays face down on the lower basket. Cups and glasses should go upside down on the upper rack. Sharp knives must be placed in the utensil holder with blades pointing down. **A** in the instructions for a dishwasher
Paula Collins investigates why so many people struggle to maintain weight loss, and speaks to leading nutrition experts about which strategies actually work long-term. **I** in an article on dieting

Draw up a detailed shopping list well in advance and check your fridge the day before... If you plan to cook an unfamiliar dish, rehearse it at least once beforehand so there are no surprises. **J** in an article on organising a dinner party
Heavily soiled or muddy items should always be washed on a separate cycle to protect more delicate fabrics from damage. **B** in the instructions for a washing machine
Please raise your glasses so that we can drink to my extraordinary grandfather, who celebrates his hundredth birthday with us today! **H** in a toast at a celebration
Gluten-Free and Dairy-Free Products. **E** on a sign in a health food shop

E.title: Word patterns (gap fill)
E.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: The whole team complimented the manager ...... her outstanding leadership during the difficult restructure.
The report is full ...... statistical errors that will need to be corrected before it is published.
I can never choose ...... a window seat and an aisle seat whenever I book a flight.
We still don't know whether the funding will come through – we'll just have to wait ...... see.
She is widely regarded ...... one of the most influential architects of the twentieth century.
Most people associate classical music ...... concert halls, formal dress codes and long evenings.
You really should be more careful ...... the personal information you share on social media.
There is a serious lack ...... affordable housing in most major cities across the country.
The proposal was well researched, but it was completely lacking ...... practical, actionable recommendations.

F.title: Verb patterns
F.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the verb in brackets.

questions.sentence: I tend not ...... (cook) at home during the working week as I usually eat at the office canteen.
Do you remember ...... (go) on that school exchange trip to Berlin when we were in Year 10?
I must remember ...... (buy) a birthday card for my sister before the shops close this evening.
Why don't you offer ...... (help) tidy up after the party instead of sitting on the sofa?
I'd suggest ...... (get) a taxi to the airport rather than trying to find a parking space.
Would you be willing ...... (lend) me your laptop while mine is being repaired at the shop?

G.title: Word formation (text)
G.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

questions.sentence: Great music production is always a strange ...... (MIX) of technical skill and artistic vision, and you certainly need to be fairly ...... (CREATE) if you want to develop a sound that is truly your own.
All recording also demands a fair amount of ...... (PREPARE) and this is doubly true when you are working on ...... (ORIGIN) compositions rather than covers.
You'll be ...... (SURPRISE) by how candidly people react when they hear your tracks for the very first time.
I've had fellow musicians tell me my latest recording is ...... (DISGUST) and ...... (THOROUGH) unlistenable!
Just smile ...... (SWEET) and thank them for taking the time to share their honest opinion.

And never forget that when you're waiting ...... (ANXIOUS) to discover whether your album will connect with listeners, and fans suddenly show their ...... (APPRECIATE), you'll know that every late night in the studio was completely worthwhile.
My tips: home- ...... (GROW) talent, developed through years of dedicated practice, always sounds the most genuine.
Every serious producer needs a professional-quality ...... (MIX). It is a fundamental piece of studio equipment, not an optional extra.
Make sure you have plenty of clearly labelled storage ...... (CONTAIN) to keep your cables, microphones and accessories organised.
And, finally, remember that pushing the boundaries of your music is not about ...... (SAFE). It is about taking creative risks and growing from every experience.
